title: penstemon humilis
intro: Penstemon humilis, also known as the Rocky Mountain Penstemon or dwarf beardtongue, is a low-growing, perennial plant native to the western United States and Canada. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which make it useful for treating wounds, reducing fever, and easing respiratory issues such as bronchitis. The plant's leaves and stems can be used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, to help promote health and well-being, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. By incorporating Penstemon humilis into a holistic approach to health, individuals may experience benefits such as improved respiratory function, reduced inflammation, and enhanced overall well-being.
